select_when: A team lacks a shared starting picture of the problem, success criteria, or the facts that later comparisons should be judged against.
Diagnosis, measurement, or ownership depends on agreeing what normal, broken, or improved actually mean.
Later intervention quality will be judged by change from an explicit starting condition rather than by anecdotes.
Work is beginning to sprawl because the goal, frame, or required output has not been clearly set.
avoid_when: The initial baseline has fossilized into doctrine even though the environment or goal has materially shifted.
The chosen baseline metric is convenient to measure but weakly tied to the real causal drivers of success or failure.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on defining the starting condition, goal, and comparison frame before analysis or execution expands.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader persona-design sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on baseline as a planning and measurement boundary.
open_questions: The source overlaps with coaching, prompting, and simulation design, but the operational file stays on baseline clarity for diagnosis and execution.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on shared starting pictures, explicit success criteria, and comparing later change against a clear baseline.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against fossilized baselines and against convenient but weak metrics.

input_type.note: The typed input label compresses the raw lack-of-baseline activation conditions.

output_type.note: The output label captures the model as setting the frame from which later comparison becomes meaningful.

reasoning_types.note: The model relies on diagnosis of the starting condition plus system-level framing of the work.
